citation: Kelly CJ, Karthikesalingam A, Suleyman M, Corrado G, King D. Key challenges for delivering clinical impact with artificial intelligence. BMC Medicine. 2019;17:195.
core_claim: Healthcare AI needs robust clinical evaluation, representative local test sets, workflow-aware metrics, regulation, and post-market surveillance.
system_design_implication: Treat model performance as one layer in a deployment system with validation, monitoring, workflow fit, and governance.

citation: Sculley D, Holt G, Golovin D, et al. Hidden technical debt in machine learning systems. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 28. 2015.
core_claim: ML systems create maintenance burdens through data dependencies, feedback loops, boundary erosion, undeclared consumers, and external-world change.
system_design_implication: Prototype outputs should expose data, model, monitoring, and consumer contracts rather than presenting a model as standalone.

citation: Wong A, Otles E, Donnelly JP, et al. External validation of a widely implemented proprietary sepsis prediction model in hospitalized patients. JAMA Internal Medicine. 2021;181(8):1065-1070.
core_claim: A widely implemented proprietary sepsis model performed substantially worse in external validation than developer-reported performance.
system_design_implication: Local validation, calibration checks, and alert burden should be treated as required deployment artifacts.

citation: Beede E, Baylor E, Hersch F, et al. A human-centered evaluation of a deep learning system deployed in clinics for the detection of diabetic retinopathy. CHI. 2020.
core_claim: Real clinical settings expose workflow, trust, infrastructure, and human factors that offline model metrics miss.
system_design_implication: Dashboard sketches should foreground workflow timing, role fit, failure modes, and local context.

citation: Liu X, Cruz Rivera S, Moher D, Calvert MJ, Denniston AK, SPIRIT-AI and CONSORT-AI Working Group. Reporting guidelines for clinical trial reports for interventions involving artificial intelligence: the CONSORT-AI extension. Nature Medicine. 2020;26:1364-1374.
core_claim: AI intervention trials need transparent reporting of intended use, setting, input/output handling, human-AI interaction, and error cases.
system_design_implication: Evaluation reports should document intended use, operational environment, user interaction, and failure cases.

citation: Rivera SC, Liu X, Chan AW, Denniston AK, Calvert MJ, SPIRIT-AI and CONSORT-AI Working Group. Guidelines for clinical trial protocols for interventions involving artificial intelligence: the SPIRIT-AI extension. Nature Medicine. 2020;26:1351-1363.
core_claim: AI trial protocols need prospective specification of intended use, data inputs, user interaction, and error handling.
system_design_implication: Prototype documentation should specify the design question and intended non-use before showing outputs.

citation: World Health Organization. Ethics and governance of artificial intelligence for health. 2021.
core_claim: Health AI governance must center ethics, human rights, accountability, and the communities affected by AI use.
system_design_implication: Repository safety labels, no-clinical-use boundaries, and governance artifacts are core design elements, not disclaimers added later.

citation: U.S. Food and Drug Administration. Framework for FDA's Real-World Evidence Program. 2018.
core_claim: RWE depends on both study methodology and the reliability and relevance of underlying real-world data.
system_design_implication: The RWE prototype must distinguish synthetic association, causal design assumptions, and data fitness.

citation: U.S. Food and Drug Administration. Proposed Regulatory Framework for Modifications to Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning-Based Software as a Medical Device. 2019.
core_claim: Adaptive AI/ML medical software requires a lifecycle approach to modifications, monitoring, and change control.
system_design_implication: Monitoring and update governance should be modeled as part of the system lifecycle.

citation: U.S. Food and Drug Administration. Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning-Based Software as a Medical Device Action Plan. 2021.
core_claim: Good machine learning practice, transparency, real-world performance monitoring, and regulatory science are central to AI/ML SaMD oversight.
system_design_implication: Evaluation registry and monitoring outputs should anticipate real-world performance evidence needs.

citation: Lund JL, Richardson DB, Sturmer T. The active comparator, new user study design in pharmacoepidemiology: historical foundations and contemporary application. Current Epidemiology Reports. 2015;2:221-228.
core_claim: Active comparator new-user designs help emulate head-to-head randomized trials and reduce common pharmacoepidemiologic biases.
system_design_implication: RWE sketches should make comparator choice, index date, eligibility, and new-user logic explicit.

citation: Hernan MA, Robins JM. Using big data to emulate a target trial when a randomized trial is not available. American Journal of Epidemiology. 2016;183(8):758-764.
core_claim: Observational analyses should be designed by first specifying the target trial they aim to emulate.
system_design_implication: RWE prototypes should start from protocol-like cohort definitions rather than model-first analysis.

citation: Rosenbaum PR, Rubin DB. The central role of the propensity score in observational studies for causal effects. Biometrika. 1983;70(1):41-55.
core_claim: The propensity score is a balancing score for observed covariates in observational causal studies under assumptions.
system_design_implication: Synthetic RWE analysis can use propensity weighting as a visible bias-reduction demonstration, while keeping causal assumptions explicit.

citation: Lipsitch M, Tchetgen Tchetgen E, Cohen T. Negative controls: a tool for detecting confounding and bias in observational studies. Epidemiology. 2010;21(3):383-388.
core_claim: Negative controls help detect residual confounding and bias when chosen with subject-matter logic.
system_design_implication: RWE reports should include negative-control outcomes as diagnostics, not as proof that estimates are causal.

citation: Bates DW, Kuperman GJ, Wang S, et al. Ten commandments for effective clinical decision support: making the practice of evidence-based medicine a reality. Journal of the American Medical Informatics Association. 2003;10(6):523-530.
core_claim: Effective CDS must fit clinical workflow, deliver support at the right time, and avoid unnecessary burden.
system_design_implication: The CDS dashboard should be workflow-timed and quiet about non-actionable information.

citation: Kawamoto K, Houlihan CA, Balas EA, Lobach DF. Improving clinical practice using clinical decision support systems: a systematic review of trials to identify features critical to success. BMJ. 2005;330:765.
core_claim: CDS success is associated with automatic support, workflow integration, and actionable recommendations at the time and location of decision-making.
system_design_implication: Prototype interfaces should be judged by workflow fit and actionability, not by score display alone.

citation: Tonekaboni S, Joshi S, McCradden MD, Goldenberg A. What clinicians want: contextualizing explainable machine learning for clinical end use. Proceedings of Machine Learning Research. 2019.
core_claim: Clinicians need context-specific explanations tied to trust, action, and clinical setting rather than generic feature rankings.
system_design_implication: Explanation panels should include caveats and uncertainty rather than only ranked feature contributions.

citation: Rudin C. Stop explaining black box machine learning models for high stakes decisions and use interpretable models instead. Nature Machine Intelligence. 2019;1:206-215.
core_claim: Post-hoc explanations can mislead in high-stakes domains when interpretable models are feasible.
system_design_implication: Prototype explanation panels should avoid implying that a feature ranking validates the model or decision.

citation: Gebru T, Morgenstern J, Vecchione B, et al. Datasheets for datasets. Communications of the ACM. 2021;64(12):86-92.
core_claim: Datasheets document dataset motivation, composition, collection, uses, and limits to improve transparency and accountability.
system_design_implication: Synthetic data generators should ship with data cards and regeneration notes.

citation: Mitchell M, Wu S, Zaldivar A, et al. Model cards for model reporting. FAT* 2019.
core_claim: Model cards document intended use, evaluation, limitations, and performance across relevant groups.
system_design_implication: Future model artifacts should include intended-use and subgroup-evaluation summaries even for synthetic prototypes.

citation: Obermeyer Z, Powers B, Vogeli C, Mullainathan S. Dissecting racial bias in an algorithm used to manage the health of populations. Science. 2019;366(6464):447-453.
core_claim: Using healthcare cost as a proxy for health need produced racial bias in population health management.
system_design_implication: Data labels and proxies must be audited before model evaluation or deployment claims are made.

citation: Vamathevan J, Clark D, Czodrowski P, et al. Applications of machine learning in drug discovery and development. Nature Reviews Drug Discovery. 2019;18:463-477.
core_claim: ML can support many drug discovery stages but depends on well-specified questions, high-quality data, repeatability, and interpretability.
system_design_implication: Pharma AI notes should emphasize evidence handoff, applicability domain, and validation rather than novelty alone.

citation: Wu Z, Ramsundar B, Feinberg EN, et al. MoleculeNet: a benchmark for molecular machine learning. Chemical Science. 2018;9:513-530.
core_claim: Standardized molecular benchmarks improve comparison but expose challenges under data scarcity, imbalance, and task complexity.
system_design_implication: Drug discovery prototypes should report calibration, applicability domain, and benchmark limitations alongside accuracy.

citation: Huang K, Fu T, Gao W, et al. Therapeutics Data Commons: machine learning datasets and tasks for drug discovery and development. NeurIPS Datasets and Benchmarks. 2021.
core_claim: Therapeutics ML needs task formulation, meaningful splits, robust generalization tests, and community benchmarks.
system_design_implication: Future pharma prototypes should use public benchmark tasks and document splits, metrics, and generalization limits.

citation: Stokes JM, Yang K, Swanson K, et al. A deep learning approach to antibiotic discovery. Cell. 2020;180(4):688-702.e13.
core_claim: Deep learning can prioritize candidates for experimental validation, but claims depend on downstream assays and biological confirmation.
system_design_implication: Case studies should separate computational prioritization from validated discovery and later development evidence.

citation: Jumper J, Evans R, Pritzel A, et al. Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold. Nature. 2021;596:583-589.
core_claim: AlphaFold reshaped structure prediction but structure accuracy is not itself proof of therapeutic utility.
system_design_implication: Drug design architecture should treat structure models as one evidence component feeding assay, ADMET, and program decisions.

citation: Vasey B, Nagendran M, Campbell B, et al. Reporting guideline for the early-stage clinical evaluation of decision support systems driven by artificial intelligence: DECIDE-AI. Nature Medicine. 2022;28:924-933.
core_claim: Early clinical evaluation of AI decision support should report actual clinical performance, safety, human factors, and small-scale deployment context.
system_design_implication: CDS and monitoring prototypes should define early evaluation artifacts before claiming workflow readiness.
